2026-04-20.content: Now available in preview, the [Looker VS Code Extension](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/getting-started-vscode-extension) brings LookML development to local desktop IDEs like Visual Studio Code, Claude Code, and Cursor. The extension supports syntax highlighting, autocomplete, real-time file synchronization with your Looker instance, and LookML validation. With the Looker MCP server, it enables [AI-assisted development](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/ai-assisted-development-vscode), allowing you to use natural language with agents like Gemini to generate and edit LookML code.

summary.title: Looker Development in VS Code
summary.summary: The new Looker VS Code Extension allows LookML development directly in your local IDE. It offers features like syntax highlighting, autocomplete, and real-time synchronization. Additionally, it enables AI-assisted development using natural language prompts for LookML generation and editing.

2026-04-15.content:
Migrate your Teams webhook to "Workflows"

Microsoft is [retiring](https://mc.merill.net/message/MC1181996) the legacy Office 365 Connectors in favor of Power Automate Workflows. Connectors will stop working by the end of April 2026. To ensure that your Looker alerts continue to deliver to Teams, you must replace your current webhook URL with a new "Workflow" URL. For more information, see the [Migrate your Teams webhook to "Workflows"](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/best-practices/migrate-teams-webhook-to-workflows) Best Practices notice.

summary.title: Teams webhook migration needed
summary.summary: Microsoft is retiring Office 365 Connectors in favor of Power Automate Workflows. To ensure Looker alerts continue to deliver to Teams, update your webhook URL to a new "Workflow" URL before the end of April 2026.

2026-04-09.content:
Starting with the Looker 26.8 release, which will release in May 2026, the following changes will occur:

* Looker (original) admins will no longer be able to access or manage the API credentials of their standard users.
* Looker (original) admins will be able to manage which users have access to individually owned API credentials.
* Users with access to API credentials will be able to access and manage their own API credentials.

Before your Looker instance is upgraded to the Looker 26.8 release, your admin must follow the steps in the [Migrating users to service accounts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-users-users#migrating) documentation page. This is to ensure admins can either create or migrate service accounts from existing standard users if they require access to users' API credentials.

For more information, see the [Discontinuing the admin capability to create, view, and manage API credentials for a standard user](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/best-practices/admin-managed-api-keys-deprecation) deprecation notice.

summary.title: Looker API Credential Management Changes
summary.summary: Starting with the Looker 26.8 release in May 2026, Looker admins will lose the ability to manage standard user API credentials. Users will gain full control over their own API credentials, and admins can manage access to individually owned credentials. Admins must migrate users to service accounts before the upgrade to ensure continued access to API credentials if needed.

2026-04-07.content:
The **Table Visualization Improvements** preview feature is now available and is disabled by default.

When this preview feature is enabled, you can use the following features for [table visualizations](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options):

* [Pagination](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options#pagination)
* [Show/Hide Table Headers](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options#row_and_header_formatting)
* [Table Borders](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options#row_and_header_formatting)
* [Cell Highlighting](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options#cell_highlighting)
* [Conditional formatting for string fields](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options#enable_conditional_formatting)
  + Note: When [Cell Highlighting](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options#cell_highlighting) is enabled, conditional formatting is not available.

summary.title: Table Visualization Improvements Now in Preview
summary.summary: Enhance your table visualizations with new features like pagination, show/hide headers, borders, and cell highlighting. This preview feature is available now and disabled by default, offering greater control over your data presentation.

2026-03-30.content: Starting March 30, 2026, the following features will begin rolling out.

summary.title: Feature Rollout Begins March 30, 2026
summary.summary: A new set of features will start rolling out to users beginning March 30, 2026. This update introduces enhancements and new capabilities designed to improve user experience and functionality. Further details on specific features will be shared closer to the release date.

2026-03-30.content: Available in preview for Looker (Google Cloud core), you can now track [end-to-end data lineage](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-lineage) from BigQuery to Looker content, including views, Explores, dashboards, and Looks, through the Looker and Dataplex lineage integration. This enables impact analysis to see how BigQuery changes affect downstream Looker (Google Cloud core) contents.

summary.title: Track Data Lineage from BigQuery to Looker
summary.summary: You can now track end-to-end data lineage from BigQuery to Looker content, including views, Explores, dashboards, and Looks, through the Looker and Dataplex lineage integration. This feature, available in preview, enables impact analysis to understand how changes in BigQuery affect downstream Looker content.

2026-03-30.content: The [Looker mobile application](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-mobile-app) now supports sending [alert notifications as push notifications](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/mobile-app-alerts) to users who have the Looker mobile application on their mobile device. (This release note was updated on April 9, 2026 to reflect that this update is a feature and to correct a documentation link.)

summary.title: Mobile alerts now available
summary.summary: Users can now receive Looker alert notifications directly as push notifications on their mobile devices. This feature requires the Looker mobile application to be installed.

2026-03-30.content: Available in preview, you can [publish the Conversational Analytics data agents](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-looker-data-agents) that you create in Looker to Gemini Enterprise.

summary.title: Conversational Analytics data agents in Looker now in preview
summary.summary: You can now publish Conversational Analytics data agents created in Looker to Gemini Enterprise. This feature is currently available in preview, allowing you to integrate your data insights more seamlessly. Explore the documentation to learn how to leverage this new capability.

2026-03-30.content: Available in preview, you can chat with [Conversational Analytics data agents](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-looker-data) in user-defined dashboards and in LookML dashboards.

summary.title: Chat with your data agents
summary.summary: You can now chat with data agents directly within user-defined and LookML dashboards. This preview feature allows for more interactive data exploration. Engage with your data like never before.

2026-03-30.content:
The [**Enhanced Content Cleanup** preview feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features#enhanced-content-cleanup) is now available. When this feature is enabled for your instance, it lets admins and content owners access an enhanced content management experience in Looker. The **Enhanced Content Cleanup** preview feature provides the following capabilities:

* Lets admins and users access a new **Unused content** folder to quickly identify and manage the unused content on a Looker instance.
* Lets admins programmatically schedule content cleanups for individual content or in bulk, and send automatic notifications to content owners.
* Lets content owners opt out of automated scheduled cleanups for specific content.
* Lets admins and users move content to the trash.

This feature is disabled by default. Learn more about [managing unused content with **Enhanced Content Cleanup**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/manage-unused-content).

summary.title: New content management tools available
summary.summary: Admins and content owners can now access enhanced tools for managing unused content within Looker. This feature allows for easier identification, cleanup scheduling, and content deletion.

2026-03-30.content: Now generally available, Looker has full support for connections with [AlloyDB for PostgreSQL](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/db-config-alloydb). When you [create a connection](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/connecting-to-your-db) in Looker, you can now select **Google Cloud AlloyDB for PostgreSQL** from the **Dialect** drop-down menu. This update doesn't affect existing AlloyDB connections that were created using the **PostgreSQL 9.5+** option in the Dialect menu.

summary.title: Looker Adds AlloyDB Support
summary.summary: Looker now fully supports connections with AlloyDB for PostgreSQL, making it easier to integrate your data. Existing connections are unaffected by this new feature.

2026-03-30.content:
The [**New Looker Explore and Merge Query Experience** preview feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features#new-looker-explore-and-merge-query-experience) is now available.

When this feature is enabled for your instance, it lets individual users have the option to try the redesigned Looker Explore and Merge Query interfaces. The streamlined interfaces let Looker users find connections and gain insights from their data more quickly. For an AI-assisted experience, enable additional options on the [**Gemini in Looker**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-platform-gil) page in the **Platform** section of the **Admin** panel.

This feature is disabled by default.

[Learn more about the new Explore and Merge Query experience](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/new-explore-experience-overview).

summary.title: New Looker Explore and Merge Query Experience Available
summary.summary: The redesigned Looker Explore and Merge Query interfaces are now available as a preview feature. This streamlined experience helps users find data connections and gain insights more quickly. For an AI-assisted experience, consider enabling Gemini in Looker.

2026-03-30.content: For [customer-hosted](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/glossary#customer-hosted) Looker instances, Looker 26.6 supports MySQL 8.4.X for the Looker backend database. For customer-hosted instances that use MySQL 8.0.X for the Looker backend database, it is recommended that you [update to MySQL 8.4.X](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/migrating-looker-backend-db-to-mysql#mysql-84x) as soon as you update your Looker instance to 26.6 or later. **Note:** This item was added on April 6, 2026.

summary.title: Looker 26.6: MySQL 8.4 Support
summary.summary: Looker 26.6 now supports MySQL 8.4.X for customer-hosted instances. It is recommended to update to MySQL 8.4.X if you are using MySQL 8.0.X for your Looker backend database when you update to version 26.6 or later.

2026-03-30.content: Available in preview, Looker (Google Cloud core) now [integrates with Dataplex Universal Catalog](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-dataplex), providing a unified discovery and management experience for your Looker metadata. This allows you to search for Looker assets like LookML models and dashboards directly within Dataplex, giving you a comprehensive view of your data landscape.

summary.title: Unified Discovery for Looker Metadata
summary.summary: Looker now integrates with Dataplex Universal Catalog, offering a unified experience for discovering and managing Looker metadata. You can now search for Looker assets, such as LookML models and dashboards, directly within Dataplex, providing a complete view of your data environment.

2026-03-30.content:
When connecting Looker to your database, you can specify [additional Java Database Connectivity (JDBC) parameters](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/connecting-to-your-db#additional_jdbc_parameters) that you want Looker to include when it communicates with your database driver. In order to keep your connection secure, Looker now has an allowlist for the additional JDBC parameters that are supported for each database dialect.

* If you try to create or update a connection that uses unsupported JDBC parameters, Looker will display an error message that shows the non-allowed parameters.
* If you have an existing connection that uses unsupported JDBC parameters, Looker will remove the parameters and then connect to your database.

For a list of the supported JDBC parameters for your dialect, see the "Supported JDBC parameters" section of the [database configuration instructions](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/dialects#database_configuration_instructions) page for your dialect.

summary.title: Secure Database Connections with JDBC Parameter Allowlist
summary.summary: Looker now enforces an allowlist for additional JDBC parameters to enhance database connection security. Unsupported parameters will be rejected or removed, ensuring a more secure and stable connection to your data.

2026-03-26.content: As part of Looker 26.6, Conversational Analytics now offers [new modes for asking questions](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-looker-data#ca-question-mode). Fast mode allows you to get answers more quickly. Thinking mode allows you to ask more complex questions and test your agent's capabilities.

summary.title: New Ways to Ask Questions in Conversational Analytics
summary.summary: Looker's Conversational Analytics now has two new modes for asking questions. Fast mode provides quicker answers, while Thinking mode enables more complex queries and exploration of your agent's abilities.

2026-03-26.content: As part of Looker 26.6, [Conversational Analytics](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-overview) will now ask you questions to clarify any ambiguities in your original query.

summary.title: Conversational Analytics Clarifies Queries
summary.summary: Looker 26.6 introduces enhanced Conversational Analytics. This new feature proactively asks clarifying questions to resolve ambiguities in your original queries, ensuring more accurate and relevant results.

summary.summary: An issue has been resolved where dashboard themes were not applying color collections correctly. This feature now functions as intended, ensuring accurate color display.

summary.summary: An issue where buttons on dashboards incorrectly added '/embed/' to link URLs has been fixed. The extension framework now functions as expected, ensuring correct URL generation for dashboard buttons.

2026-03-25.content: An issue has been fixed where the font and background color picker was not accessible when you edited visualizations on merge queries. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.summary: An issue where opening the Interaction Details dialog in a new window resulted in a 401 error has been resolved. The feature now functions correctly.

summary.summary: An issue where pressing Enter to confirm IME composition prematurely submitted messages in Conversational Analytics has been resolved. The feature now functions as intended, ensuring messages are not submitted until explicitly sent.

2026-03-25.content: An issue has been fixed where the LookML Assistant could return a 404 error. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.summary: The LookML Assistant previously returned a 404 error for some requests. This issue has now been fixed, and the feature is functioning correctly.

summary.summary: An issue causing automatic tab additions to dashboards has been fixed. Dashboards will now function as expected without unintended tab insertions.

2026-03-25.content: An issue has been fixed where visualization tooltips on dashboards could use incorrect background or text colors. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.summary: An issue with dashboard visualization tooltips displaying incorrect colors has been resolved. The tooltips will now show the expected background and text colors, ensuring a better user experience.

2026-03-25.content: An issue has been fixed where the **LookML dashboards** folder could fail to display the complete list of LookML dashboards. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: LookML dashboards folder display fixed
summary.summary: An issue preventing the full display of LookML dashboards in their folder has been resolved. The feature now functions correctly, showing all available dashboards as expected.

2026-03-25.content: Tabbed dashboards with unsupported layouts will now display a warning message prompting users to [update to a new layout](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/best-practices/troubleshooting-unsupported-dashboard-layout).

summary.title: Dashboard layout warning updated
summary.summary: Unsupported dashboard layouts will now show a warning. Users are prompted to update to a new layout to ensure proper display and functionality.

2026-03-23.content:
To enhance security, the [Looker language SDKs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/api-sdk) and the Looker API [`/login` endpoint](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/reference/looker-api/latest/methods/ApiAuth/login) are being modified. They will exclusively accept passing credentials in the HTTP request body and will no longer support using URL query parameters.

**Release date**: This update is expected to take effect with the Looker 26.18 release in October 2026.

**Potential impact**: Any scripts or applications currently passing credentials in the URL query parameters in the Looker SDK libraries, or directly calling the `/login` API endpoint, will fail after this update.

**Who is affected**: All customers using Looker SDKs, custom scripts, or applications that call the `/login` API endpoint directly.

**Action required**:

We have sent a message to your affected customers. However, to help avoid service disruptions, please recommend that they evaluate their environment and take the following actions before October 2026:

* **Upgrade SDKs:** Upgrade the Looker SDKs to version 26.4 or later as soon as possible.
* **Update custom scripts:** Modify any scripts or applications that rely on passing Looker API credentials in URL query parameters so that they will pass credentials in the HTTP request body.
* **Test the environment:** Validate these changes in an environment that can identify these potential misconfigurations.

summary.title: Looker API authentication update
summary.summary: Looker is updating its API authentication to enhance security. Starting with the 26.18 release in October 2026, credentials will only be accepted in the HTTP request body, not as URL query parameters. Customers should upgrade SDKs or update custom scripts to avoid service disruptions.

2026-03-20.content:
A new version of the Looker mobile app is available for iOS (version 2.2.0) and Android (version 2.0.88). The mobile app now includes the following features and improvements:

* The app supports tabbed dashboards.
* You can open dashboard links directly in the app.
* Load time has been improved.
* Dashboard opening zoom bugs and other issues have been fixed.

summary.title: Looker Mobile App Update
summary.summary: The Looker mobile app for iOS and Android has been updated with new features, including support for tabbed dashboards and direct dashboard link opening. This release also brings performance improvements and bug fixes.

2026-03-17.content: As part of Looker 26.4, the following features will begin rolling out on March 17, 2026.

summary.title: Looker 26.4 Features Rolling Out March 17
summary.summary: Looker 26.4 is introducing new features starting March 17, 2026. This update aims to enhance the Looker platform with improved functionalities. Further details on the specific enhancements will be available upon release.

2026-03-17.content: Looker **Connections Settings** page has a new option, [**Disable Connection**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/connecting-to-your-db#disable_connection), that allows a Looker admin to disable a connection in cases where there are downstream issues with the database, instead of killing queries manually or allowing queries to remain in the query queue. When the connection is disabled, Looker will not send queries to the database and will return an error message to users.

summary.title: Disable Database Connections in Looker
summary.summary: Looker admins can now disable database connections directly from the Connections Settings page. This prevents Looker from sending queries to the database, avoiding manual query killing and reducing the risk of downstream issues.

2026-03-17.content: The [**Visualization Assistant**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/custom-looker-visualization-gemini), which lets you customize formatting options for Looker visualizations in natural language with Gemini assistance, is now generally available. This assistant can be enabled by turning on the **Gemini in Looker** and **Looker Assistants** settings on the **Gemini in Looker** page in the **Platform** section of the **Admin** panel.

summary.title: Customize Looker Visualizations with Natural Language
summary.summary: The Visualization Assistant, powered by Gemini, is now generally available. This feature allows users to customize Looker visualization formatting using natural language prompts. Enable it through the Admin panel's Gemini in Looker settings.

2026-03-17.content:
The [**Self-service Explores**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/exploring-self-service) feature is now generally available.

In addition, if your Looker admin has [enabled your Looker instance to support OAuth for Google Sheets uploads](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-self-service-explore#enabling_oauth_for_uploads), the self-service Explores feature supports [uploading data from Google Sheets using Google Drive navigation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/exploring-self-service#oauth).

summary.title: Self-service Explores Now Available
summary.summary: The self-service Explores feature is now generally available, allowing users to explore data more freely. Additionally, with OAuth enabled, you can now upload data directly from Google Sheets using Google Drive navigation.

2026-03-17.content:
The [content certification](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/content-certification) feature is now generally available. In Looker 26.4, the following support has been added:

* LookML Explores can now be certified.
* Looker admins can now automatically certify all current and future LookML dashboards and LookML Explores.
* When [Enhanced Search](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/finding-content#enhanced_search) is enabled, searching for content now includes the ability to sort and filter search results based on certification status.

summary.title: Content Certification Now Generally Available
summary.summary: The content certification feature is now generally available, allowing LookML Explores and dashboards to be certified. Admins can automate certification, and users can filter search results by certification status with enhanced search.

2026-03-17.content:
The [Continuous Integration (CI)](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/continuous-integration) feature is updated for better integration with the Looker user interface:

* Your Looker instance will be migrated to the new version of Looker CI. The migration will take a few minutes.
* While your instance is migrating, any existing CI suites will not appear on the CI [**Suites**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/ci-create-suite#creating_a_ci_suite) page, and the history on the CI [**Runs**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/ci-view-results) page will be empty. During this time, do not create new suites or initiate manual runs.
* When the migration is complete, the **Suites** and **Runs** pages will be repopulated.
* If you encounter an issue during the migration, please report it using the [Looker Continuous Integration - Preview Issue Tracker form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSed30eICYruU45R8WZQH6hIui28xtdDnejfetzpLHQ5PK0ZeA/viewform?usp=dialog).

The updated Looker Continuous Integration feature includes the following:

* Your Looker admin no longer needs to manually add CI users. After your Looker instance is [enabled for CI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/continuous-integration#enable-ci) (or once your instance is updated to the new CI, if your instance was already enabled for CI), Looker automatically creates ten Looker CI users. The Looker CI users are created in the [Looker CI Users user group](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-users-groups#ci-users) with the [Looker CI Users role](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-users-roles#ci-users). A Looker admin can view the Looker CI users from the **Service Accounts** tab of the [**Users**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-users-users) Admin page.
* A new [**GitHub** table](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-platform-ci#github-table) on the **Continuous Integration** Admin page lists the GitHub repositories that have been granted access to the Looker CI GitHub application, which enables Looker CI to [be triggered automatically when pull requests are created](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/ci-create-suite#triggers).

**Note:** This item was updated on March 27, 2026.

summary.title: Looker CI Enhancements Released
summary.summary: The Continuous Integration (CI) feature in Looker has been updated for improved user interface integration. This update includes automatic creation of CI users and a new GitHub table for enhanced repository management. The migration process is automated and should take only a few minutes to complete.

2026-03-17.content:
The **Dashboard Tile Limits** preview feature is now available, and is disabled by default.

When enabled, the **Dashboard Tile Limits** preview feature lets admins set limits to how many query tiles can be added to each tab in a dashboard. Admins can set these limits in the **Maximum Query Tiles per tab** setting on the **Content guardrails** admin page.

summary.title: Dashboard Tile Limits Preview Now Available
summary.summary: Admins can now set limits on the number of query tiles per dashboard tab with the new Dashboard Tile Limits preview feature. This feature, disabled by default, can be configured on the Content guardrails admin page.

2026-03-17.content: The Gemini in Looker assistant that helps you [generate LookML parameters](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/write-lookml-gemini) is now available to enable individually from other Gemini in Looker features. This assistant, which continues to be available in preview, suggests LookML parameters that are based on the natural language prompts that you add to your project files. This assistant can be enabled by turning on the **Gemini in Looker** and **LookML Assistant** settings on the **Gemini in Looker** page in the **Platform** section of the **Admin** panel.

summary.title: LookML Assistant Now Available Individually
summary.summary: The Gemini in Looker assistant for generating LookML parameters can now be enabled separately from other Gemini features. This preview assistant suggests LookML based on natural language prompts and can be activated in the Admin panel. Ensure both 'Gemini in Looker' and 'LookML Assistant' settings are enabled.

2026-03-17.content:
The [**Increased Row Limit** preview feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features#increased-row-limit) feature is now available, and is disabled by default.

When enabled, the **Increased Row Limit** preview feature lets admins set row limits up to 50,000 rows or datapoints for [map charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/google-map-options), [scatterplot charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/scatter-options), and [table charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options). Admins can set the limits for each visualization type in the [**Visualization limits**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-performance-center-content-guardrails#visualization-limits) setting on the **Content guardrails** admin page.

Table charts, scatterplot charts, and Google Maps charts that have increased row limits are subject to additional limitations when you download, send, or schedule dashboards in PDF format. See [Downloading or delivering dashboards in rendered formats](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/best-practices/rendered-format-options-downloading-delivering-dashboards) for more information about scheduling and downloading dashboard PDFs with increased row limits.

**Note:** This item was originally published on March 16, 2026 and was updated on March 17, 2026.

summary.title: Increased Row Limit for Charts Now Available
summary.summary: Admins can now enable the Increased Row Limit preview feature to set row limits up to 50,000 for map, scatterplot, and table charts. This feature, disabled by default, allows for greater data visualization capacity but may introduce additional limitations when downloading dashboards in PDF format.

2026-03-17.content: Now available in preview, you can use Gemini in Looker to [assist you in writing Looker expressions](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/gemini-expression-asst) for [table calculations](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-calculations) and [custom fields](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/custom-fields). This feature can be enabled by turning on the **Expression Assistant** setting on the **Gemini in Looker** page in the **Platform** section of the **Admin** panel.

summary.title: Gemini Assists Looker Expression Writing
summary.summary: Gemini is now available in preview to help you write Looker expressions for table calculations and custom fields. This feature, called Expression Assistant, can be enabled in the Admin panel. It aims to simplify the process of creating complex data manipulations within Looker.

2026-03-17.content:
The [**Enhanced Search** preview feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features#enhanced-search) is now available.

When enabled, [Enhanced Search](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/finding-content#enhanced_search) helps you find saved content on your instance using Gemini in Looker. Enhanced Search moves beyond keyword matching to interpreting the conceptual meaning of your search queries, letting you search for saved content using business terms or analytical questions (for example, "total customer acquisition cost").

You can filter your search results to limit them to specific content types, folders, content creators, creation dates, modification dates, and the content certification status.

To enable Gemini in Looker assistance with searching content, you must also enable the **Semantic Search** setting on the **Gemini in Looker** page in the **Platform** section of the **Admin** panel.

**Note:** The **Semantic Search** option will begin rolling out on April 6, 2026. This item was updated on April 2, 2026.

summary.title: Enhanced Search is now available
summary.summary: Find saved content more easily with Enhanced Search, powered by Gemini in Looker. This feature interprets the meaning of your search queries, allowing you to use business terms and analytical questions for more accurate results.

2026-03-17.content:
Now available in preview, Gemini in Looker can automatically [generate Quick Start analyses for Explores](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/gemini-quick-starts). This feature can be enabled by turning on the **AI-assisted Quick Starts** setting on the **Gemini in Looker** page in the **Platform** section of the **Admin** panel.

**Note:** This feature is not yet available. This item was modified on March 19, 2026.

summary.title: New Quick Start Analyses with Gemini
summary.summary: Gemini in Looker now offers a preview of Quick Start analyses for Explores. This feature, which can be enabled in the Admin panel, helps users get started with their data more easily.

2026-03-17.content:
Available in preview, you can now use custom calendars, such as fiscal or retail calendars, for [dialects that support it](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/custom-calendars#supported_dialects_for_custom_calendars). The [custom calendar](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/custom-calendars) feature lets your Looker developers create a LookML model of a custom calendar table in your database and then create [custom calendar dimension groups](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/reference/param-field-dimension-group#custom_calendar_type) that use the custom calendar. Your end users can then create Explore queries using the custom calendar dimension timeframes.

In addition, your LookML developers can create [period-over-period](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/period-over-period) measures based on custom calendar dimension groups.

**Note:** This item was updated on March 19, 2026.

summary.title: Use Custom Calendars in Looker
summary.summary: Looker now supports custom calendars, allowing developers to model fiscal or retail calendars in LookML. This enables end users to create Explore queries and period-over-period measures based on these custom timeframes.

2026-03-17.content: Conversational Analytics now offers [new modes for asking questions](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-looker-data#ca-question-mode). Fast mode allows you to get answers more quickly. Thinking mode allows you to ask more complex questions and test your agent's capabilities. (Note: Question modes are not yet available. This release note was updated on March 24, 2026.)

summary.title: New question modes for Conversational Analytics
summary.summary: Conversational Analytics has introduced new modes for asking questions, including Fast mode for quicker answers and Thinking mode for more complex queries. These modes will enhance user interaction and agent testing capabilities, although they are not yet available.

2026-03-12.content:
Looker (Google Cloud core) instances with public or hybrid connections now support [IP allowlists](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-ip-allowlist), which enhance security by ensuring that only traffic from specified IP addresses can access your instance. To connect to certain Google Cloud services, like Conversational Analytics or Connected Sheets, you can [select an option](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-ip-allowlist#allowlist-services) to automatically allowlist the necessary IP ranges for those services. You can configure an IP allowlist for an existing instance by editing it in the Google Cloud console.

This feature will roll out to instances over the next week.

summary.title: Looker IP allowlisting is now available
summary.summary: Enhance the security of your Looker instance with IP allowlisting. This feature restricts access to only approved IP addresses, ensuring that only authorized traffic can reach your instance. You can configure IP allowlisting through the Google Cloud console.

2026-03-12.content:
The [**Granular Dashboard Sizing** preview feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features#granular-dashboard-sizing) is temporarily unavailable for some Looker instances. Any dashboard tiles that were resized during this feature's enablement will revert to Looker's original sizing constraints.

(**Note**: This release note was added on March 16, 2026.)

summary.title: Dashboard Sizing Feature Temporarily Unavailable
summary.summary: The granular dashboard sizing preview feature is currently unavailable for some Looker instances. Dashboards that were resized using this feature will revert to their original sizing.

2026-03-11.content: Beginning in Looker 26.4, customer-hosted Looker instances will use a new LookML parser with optimized performance. This parser is already in use for Looker-hosted instances. For customer-hosted instances on Looker 26.4, if you want to revert to the legacy parser, contact Looker Support for details on how to disable the new parser. The legacy parser will be fully deprecated in Looker 26.6.

summary.title: New LookML Parser for Customer-Hosted Instances
summary.summary: Looker 26.4 introduces a new, high-performance LookML parser for customer-hosted instances, mirroring the parser already used in Looker-hosted instances. While you can revert to the legacy parser by contacting Looker Support, it will be fully deprecated in Looker 26.6.

2026-03-09.content: It is no longer necessary to [manually configure OAuth credentials](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-create-oauth) when creating a Looker (Google Cloud core) instance that uses only public secure connections and no custom domain. For these instances, Looker (Google Cloud core) now automatically manages OAuth client and secret information, simplifying the setup process. Manual OAuth configuration is still required for instances that use private connections, hybrid connections, or a custom domain.

summary.title: Simplified Looker OAuth Setup
summary.summary: Looker (Google Cloud core) now automatically manages OAuth credentials for instances using public secure connections and no custom domain, removing the need for manual configuration. This streamlined process simplifies instance setup. Manual OAuth configuration remains necessary for instances with private, hybrid, or custom domain connections.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where dashboard buttons that open links in a new tab could fail to work properly when cookieless embed was enabled. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Dashboard buttons now work with cookieless embed
summary.summary: An issue where dashboard buttons failed to open links in a new tab when cookieless embed was enabled has been resolved. This functionality now works as expected.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where certain tables were being unnecessarily truncated when they were downloaded to PDF. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Table download to PDF fixed
summary.summary: An issue causing unnecessary table truncation during PDF downloads has been resolved. This feature now functions correctly.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where the "raw" timeframe of a `dimension_group` could be displayed in a 12-hour format instead of the expected 24-hour format. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Timeframe display corrected
summary.summary: An issue has been fixed where the 'raw' timeframe of a dimension group could be displayed in a 12-hour format instead of the expected 24-hour format. The feature now performs as expected.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where dimension fill could fail to populate values when the row limit is reached. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Dimension fill now populates values correctly
summary.summary: An issue that prevented dimension fill from populating values when the row limit was reached has been resolved. The feature now functions as expected, ensuring accurate data population.

2026-03-05.content:
Full support for connections with [AlloyDB for PostgreSQL](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/db-config-alloydb) is available in preview. When you [create a connection](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/connecting-to-your-db) in Looker, you can now select **Google Cloud AlloyDB for PostgreSQL** from the Dialect drop-down menu. This update does not affect existing AlloyDB connections that were created using the **PostgreSQL 9.5+** option in the Dialect menu.

**Note:** This item was updated March 9, 2026.

summary.title: AlloyDB for PostgreSQL connections now in preview
summary.summary: Looker now offers full support for connections with AlloyDB for PostgreSQL, available in preview. You can select this new dialect when creating new database connections in Looker. Existing connections made with the PostgreSQL 9.5+ option will not be affected.

2026-03-05.content:
Dashboard filter enhancements are now generally available. Dashboard filter enhancements include the following improvements:

* Persistent filter suggestion drop-down: Filter suggestion drop-downs remain open for easier multi-selection for [tag list](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/filters-user-defined-dashboards#tag-list) and [advanced filter](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/filters-user-defined-dashboards#advanced_filters) type filters.
* Select or deselect all filter values: Lets users select or deselect all values in tag list and advanced filter type filters.
* Limit condition controls for advanced filters: Lets dashboard creators limit the condition options that are available to users for advanced filters.
* Enable [**Include custom filter values**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/presenting-content#adding_dashboards_to_boards_with_custom_filter_values_applied) by default for boards: Admins can choose to enable Include custom filter values for boards by default instance-wide.

summary.title: Dashboard filter enhancements available
summary.summary: Dashboard filter enhancements are now generally available, offering improved usability with persistent suggestions, select/deselect all options, and more control for filter creators. Admins can also enable custom filter values by default for boards.

2026-03-05.content:
[Tabbed dashboards](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/tabbed-dashboards) are now generally available. Dashboard editors can use tabs to organize dashboard content across multiple tabs within a single dashboard. Adding tabs to dashboards lets you do the following:

* Better organize content: Group related visualizations and tiles into separate tabs.
* Improve data storytelling: Guide viewers through different aspects of your data in a structured way.
* Enhance performance: Load only the tiles on an active tab, potentially speeding up initial dashboard load times.
* Reduce clutter: Consolidate multiple related analyses into a single dashboard.

summary.title: Organize dashboards with tabs
summary.summary: Tabbed dashboards are now generally available, allowing dashboard editors to organize content across multiple tabs within a single dashboard. This feature helps improve content organization, data storytelling, performance, and reduces clutter by consolidating related analyses.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where dashboard table tiles could fail to display the scroll bar and total row. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Dashboard table scroll bar and total row issue fixed
summary.summary: An issue where dashboard table tiles failed to display the scroll bar and total row has been resolved. This functionality now operates correctly.

2026-03-05.content:
The **Labs** page in the **Admin** panel has been renamed **Preview Features**. The new page organizes in-progress features into two categories to provide better clarity on their status:

* **Preview features**: These features have a planned path to general availability.
* **Labs features**: These experimental features may be removed from the product at any time, and issues may not be resolved.

Your enablement status for each feature has not changed. For more information, see the [Preview Features](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features) documentation page.

summary.title: Preview Features page replaces Labs
summary.summary: The Labs page in the Admin panel is now called Preview Features. This new page categorizes in-progress features into 'Preview features' and 'Labs features' for better clarity on their status. Your enablement status for each feature remains unchanged.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where Looker could fail to retain sorts when content from a drill modal was being downloaded. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Sorts retained on drill download
summary.summary: An issue has been fixed where Looker failed to retain sorts when content from a drill modal was downloaded. This functionality now operates as expected.

2026-03-05.content:
The Looker API now includes Looker [Conversational Analytics endpoints](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/reference/looker-api/latest/methods/ConversationalAnalytics) that you can use with applications that embed Looker to create and manage agents, conversations, and messages.

For tips on using the Conversational Analytics endpoints, see the [Best practices for using the ConversationalAnalytics API endpoints in Looker's API](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/best-practices/ca-apis-in-looker-api-best-practices) documentation page.

**Note:** This item was updated March 13, 2026.

summary.title: Looker Adds Conversational Analytics API
summary.summary: Looker's API now supports Conversational Analytics endpoints. These endpoints allow you to build and manage agents, conversations, and messages within embedded Looker applications. This feature enhances interactive data exploration and analysis capabilities.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where selecting the **Expand tables** option during a PDF download or schedule could cause the tables to shrink instead of expand. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Table expansion in PDF downloads fixed
summary.summary: The issue where the 'Expand tables' option caused tables to shrink during PDF downloads or schedules has been resolved. The feature now correctly expands tables as intended.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where pivoted columns could fail to be sorted correctly when content was being downloaded. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Column sorting fix for downloads
summary.summary: An issue that caused pivoted columns to sort incorrectly during content downloads has been resolved. The feature now functions as expected, ensuring correct sorting when downloading content.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where conditional formatting rules could fail to be saved. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting save issue resolved
summary.summary: A bug preventing conditional formatting rules from being saved has been fixed. The feature now functions correctly, allowing users to save their formatting rules without errors.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where LookML dashboards could not be created using the `ProjectFile` API endpoint. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: LookML dashboard creation via ProjectFile API fixed
summary.summary: An issue preventing LookML dashboard creation through the ProjectFile API has been resolved. The ProjectFile API endpoint now functions correctly for creating LookML dashboards.

2026-03-05.content:
Custom tooltips are now generally available and supported for more visualization types. Custom tooltips let you configure tooltips within the [Explore visualization editor](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/creating-visualizations#quick_guide) by using a combination of UI settings and an HTML editor that supports Liquid templating. Custom tooltips are supported for most visualization types except the following:

* [Donut multiples charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/donut-multiples-options)
* [Word cloud charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/word-cloud-options)
* [Static map (regions) charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/map-regions-options)
* [Static maps (points) charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/map-points-options)
* Charts made using the Chart Config Editor (with the exception of [Sankey charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/sankey))
* Custom visualizations

[Learn more about custom tooltips](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/custom-tooltips).

summary.title: Custom tooltips now generally available
summary.summary: Custom tooltips are now generally available, allowing you to configure tooltips for most visualization types using UI settings and Liquid templating. This feature enhances the interactivity and information displayed in your visualizations.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where Looker would prevent users from saving changes on the Content Guardrails admin settings page. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Content Guardrails Save Issue Resolved
summary.summary: An issue preventing users from saving changes on the Content Guardrails admin settings page has been fixed. The feature now functions correctly, allowing users to save their configurations without interruption.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where Looker could fail to recognize non-numeric values in conditional formatting rules for single value visualizations. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting for single value visualizations fixed
summary.summary: An issue where Looker failed to recognize non-numeric values in conditional formatting rules for single value visualizations has been resolved. The feature now functions correctly.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where entering Japanese characters into advanced filters could add extra characters. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Japanese character input in filters fixed
summary.summary: An issue where Japanese characters were incorrectly added to advanced filters has been resolved. The filtering feature now functions correctly.

2026-03-05.content:
**Looker 26.4** is expected to include the following changes, features, and fixes:

* Expected Looker (original) deployment start: **Sunday, March 8, 2026**
* Expected Looker (original) final deployment and download available: **Thursday, March 19, 2026**
* Expected Looker (Google Cloud core) deployment start: **Monday, March 16, 2026**
* Expected Looker (Google Cloud core) final deployment: **Monday, March 23, 2026**

summary.title: Looker 26.4 Release Details
summary.summary: The Looker 26.4 release is scheduled for March 2026, with deployments for both Looker (original) and Looker (Google Cloud core) beginning in the second week of the month. Final deployments and downloads will be available by March 19th for the original version and March 23rd for the Google Cloud core version.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where dashboards that used ClickHouse connections could fail to load with a `ClassCastException` error. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Dashboards with ClickHouse connections now load correctly
summary.summary: An issue that caused dashboards using ClickHouse connections to fail loading with a ClassCastException error has been resolved. Dashboards with ClickHouse connections now function as expected.

2026-03-05.content:
The [Embed Conversational Analytics](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-looker-embedding) feature is now generally available. In Looker 26.4, the following support has been added:

* Embedding Conversational Analytics is now supported on Looker (Google Cloud core).
* The Looker [embed SDK](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/embed-sdk-demo) now supports embedded Conversational Analytics.
* [Embedded themes](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/themes-for-embedded-dashboards-and-explores) are now partially supported with embedded Conversational Analytics. All of the [General options](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/themes-for-embedded-dashboards-and-explores#general) for themes are supported. All other theme options are not supported.

summary.title: Conversational Analytics Now Generally Available
summary.summary: Conversational Analytics is now generally available. This feature supports embedding within Looker (Google Cloud core) and the Looker embed SDK. Additionally, general theme options are now partially supported for embedded experiences.

2026-03-05.content: An issue has been fixed where comma-separated negative filters could be incorrectly interpreted with OR logic in the filter UI. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Filter UI Logic Corrected
summary.summary: The filter UI now correctly interprets comma-separated negative filters, resolving an issue where they were previously treated with OR logic. This fix ensures the feature performs as expected.

2026-03-05.content:
The **Gemini in Looker** page in the **Admin** panel has been redesigned to allow Looker admins to selectively enable Gemini in Looker features. For more information about how to enable the features that are available for each Looker instance type, see the following documentation:

* Looker (Google Cloud core): [Administer Gemini on your Looker (Google Cloud core) instance](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-admin-gemini)
* Looker: [Admin settings – Gemini in Looker](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-platform-gil)

summary.title: New Gemini in Looker Admin Settings
summary.summary: Looker admins can now selectively enable Gemini features through a redesigned Admin panel page. This provides more granular control over Gemini's capabilities within your Looker instance.

2026-03-05.content: Project-level local git hooks are no longer supported for security reasons. This change will have no effect on Looker-hosted instances, but it may affect customer-hosted instances depending on your Git setup.

summary.title: Local Git Hooks No Longer Supported
summary.summary: Project-level local Git hooks are deprecated for security reasons. This impacts customer-hosted instances; Looker-hosted instances are unaffected. Please review your Git setup if you are using customer-hosted instances.

2026-03-05.content:
The [Preview Features page](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features) is now available in the **Admin** panel for Looker (Google Cloud core). This page lets you enable or disable new features that are currently in preview.

The following preview features are now available in Looker (Google Cloud core):

* Access Content Certification
* Granular Dashboard Sizing
* Internal Dashboard Theming
* Tile Download Default Options

Preview features that are categorized as "Labs" on the **Preview Features** page of the **Admin** panel aren't supported in Looker (Google Cloud core).

summary.title: Manage Preview Features in Admin Panel
summary.summary: You can now enable or disable new preview features directly from the Admin panel in Looker. Access Content Certification, Granular Dashboard Sizing, Internal Dashboard Theming, and Tile Download Default Options are now available for preview.

2026-03-02.content: You can now [manage backups](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/looker-core-backup-restore) for Looker (Google Cloud core) instances in the Google Cloud console. From the new **Backups** tab on the instance details page, you can create manual backups, and you can view, restore, or delete both automatic and manual backups. Previously, these operations were only available through the gcloud command-line tool.

summary.title: Manage Looker Backups in Google Cloud Console
summary.summary: You can now manage backups for Looker instances directly within the Google Cloud console. The new Backups tab allows you to create, view, restore, and delete both automatic and manual backups, simplifying instance management.

2026-02-17.content: Now available in preview, the **Self-service Explores** feature supports [uploading data from Google Sheets](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/exploring-self-service#from-sheets). To support uploading data from Google Sheets, your Looker admin must [enable the required APIs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-self-service-explore#enable-apis) in the Google Cloud project that houses your BigQuery database.

summary.title: Self-service Explores now supports Google Sheets
summary.summary: Self-service Explores is now available in preview, allowing you to upload data directly from Google Sheets. To use this feature, your Looker admin needs to enable specific APIs in your Google Cloud project.

2026-02-17.content:
Looker admins can grant the new [`schedule_without_limit` permission](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-users-roles#schedule_without_limit) to restrict a user's ability to [deliver all data results from a Look or an Explore](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/delivering-looks-explores). This permission must be combined with another Looker permission that permits delivering data.

This permission will be automatically granted to any existing roles that already contain a data delivery permission. Looker admins must remove this permission from any users whom they wish to prevent from delivering all results from a Look or an Explore.

**Note:** This item was added on February 19, 2026.

summary.title: New scheduling permission in Looker
summary.summary: Looker admins can now use the schedule_without_limit permission to control user access to delivering all data results from Looks or Explores. This permission will be automatically applied to existing roles with data delivery capabilities. Admins can manually remove it from specific users to restrict their ability to deliver all results.

2026-02-17.content: Now available in preview, dashboard editors can change the size and layout of dashboard tiles with more granularity. To enable this feature, a Looker admin must turn on the [**Granular Dashboard Sizing** Labs feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-labs#granular-dashboard-sizing).

summary.title: Dashboard Tiles Resizing
summary.summary: Dashboard editors can now adjust the size and layout of tiles with greater precision. This new preview feature requires a Looker admin to enable Granular Dashboard Sizing in the Labs section of the Admin panel.

2026-02-17.content: Now available in preview, the [**Embed Conversational Analytics** Labs feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-labs#embed_conversational_analytics) is now available. When enabled, the Embed Conversational Analytics Labs feature lets you [embed Conversational Analytics conversations and agents in an iframe](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-looker-embedding) like other Looker content types.

summary.title: Conversational Analytics Embedding Now in Preview
summary.summary: You can now embed Conversational Analytics conversations and agents within an iframe. This new preview feature allows for seamless integration of conversational data into your applications, similar to other Looker content.

2026-02-17.content: The following features will roll out over a four-day period starting February 17, 2026.

summary.title: New features coming soon
summary.summary: New features will be gradually released starting February 17, 2026. This rollout will take place over a four-day period.

2026-02-17.content:
Now available in preview, LookML developers can unlock their Git branch in cases where their Git branch is locked as a result of another Git operation in progress or a previous Git operation failing. When the Git repository is locked, Looker displays the **Unlock Branch** option in the [**Git Actions** panel](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/git-command-reference#git_actions_panel) of the Looker IDE. In addition, if a LookML developer tries to commit a change on a locked Git branch, Looker displays a warning in the **Commit** dialog, along with an option to delete the Git lock. See the [Using version control and deploying](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/version-control-and-deploying-changes#unlock-branch) documentation page for details.

**Note:** This item was added on February 18, 2026.

summary.title: Unlock Git Branch in Looker
summary.summary: LookML developers can now unlock their Git branches in Looker, resolving issues caused by in-progress or failed Git operations. The 'Unlock Branch' option is available in the Git Actions panel, and warnings with an option to delete the lock appear when committing changes on a locked branch.

2026-02-17.content:
The [content certification](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/content-certification) feature, already available in preview, includes the following updates:

* The [**Settings**](admin-panel-general-settings) page in the **General** section of the **Admin** panel now includes options that let admins enable or disable content certification, set a custom URL for the certification process, and control whether certification is revoked when content is edited.
* LookML dashboards can now be certified.
* Looks and dashboards that are based on uncertified self-service Explores now display the "ungoverned" badge.
* Searching for content now includes the ability to sort content based on its certification status. (**Note**: This capability is not yet available. This item was modified on February 26, 2026.)

summary.title: Content Certification Updates
summary.summary: The content certification feature has been updated with new administrative controls, the ability to certify LookML dashboards, and enhanced search capabilities. Admins can now manage certification settings, and users will see an 'ungoverned' badge on content derived from uncertified Explores.

2026-02-17.content: Now available in preview, the **In-database merge queries** feature is supported for [merging results](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/merged-results) of two queries that are on the same BigQuery connection. Previously, the join for merging results was always performed in Looker memory, which limited each query to 5,000 rows that could be calculated in the join. If your Looker admin has enabled the [**In-database merge queries** Looker Labs feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-labs#in-database-merge-queries), the join between two queries that are on the same BigQuery connection is performed in the BigQuery database itself. Performing the join in the database is more performant and allows for unlimited rows that can be joined.

summary.title: In-database merge queries now in preview
summary.summary: The new In-database merge queries feature, now in preview, allows for more performant merging of query results directly within BigQuery. This eliminates previous row limitations and improves the efficiency of data joins.

2026-02-17.content: Now available in preview, dashboard editors can [set default options for how the download of a dashboard tile includes query results](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/editing-user-defined-dashboards#download-limit). They can set the download to include by default all query results or the current result table that is displayed in the visualization, or set a custom number of rows and columns. To enable this feature, a Looker admin must turn on the [**Tile Download Default Options** Labs feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-labs#dashboard-tile-download-limits).

summary.title: Set default dashboard tile download options
summary.summary: Dashboard editors can now set default options for downloading query results from dashboard tiles. This feature, available in preview, allows setting defaults for all results, the current table, or a custom number of rows and columns. Looker admins need to enable the 'Tile Download Default Options' Labs feature.

2026-02-17.content: Now available in preview, the **Self-service Explores** feature supports updating the data for a self-service Explore. The owner of a self-service Explore can update it with data from the latest version of the file that was used to create the self-service Explore. See the [Creating self-service Explores](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/exploring-self-service#update-data) documentation page for more information.

summary.title: Update data for self-service Explores
summary.summary: Self-service Explores now support updating data with the latest file version. This feature, currently in preview, allows owners to refresh their Explores easily. More details can be found in the documentation on creating self-service Explores.

2026-02-17.content: The [System Activity **User Activity** dashboard](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/system-activity-dashboards#user_activity_dashboard) has been updated to improve authentication troubleshooting. New information includes recent login failures, the authentication method that was used, the error message that was returned, and the time of the attempt.

summary.title: New dashboard for authentication troubleshooting
summary.summary: The User Activity dashboard has been updated with new information to help you troubleshoot authentication issues. You can now see recent login failures, the authentication method used, error messages, and the time of each attempt.

2026-03-16.content:
The [**Increased Row Limit** preview feature](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-general-preview-features#increased-row-limit) feature is now available, and is disabled by default.

When enabled, the **Increased Row Limit** preview feature lets admins set row limits up to 50,000 rows or datapoints for [map charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/google-map-options), [scatterplot charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/scatter-options), and [table charts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/table-options). Admins can set the limits for each visualization type in the [**Visualization limits**](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-performance-center-content-guardrails#visualization-limits) setting on the **Content guardrails** admin page.

Table charts, scatterplot charts, and Google Maps charts that have increased row limits are subject to additional limitations when you download, send, or schedule dashboards in PDF format. See [Downloading or delivering dashboards in rendered formats](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/best-practices/rendered-format-options-downloading-delivering-dashboards) for more information about scheduling and downloading dashboard PDFs with increased row limits.

summary.title: Increased Row Limit Feature Available
summary.summary: The Increased Row Limit preview feature is now available. This feature allows admins to set higher row limits for map, scatterplot, and table charts, up to 50,000 rows or datapoints. Be aware of additional limitations when downloading or scheduling dashboards with these increased limits in PDF format.

2026-03-16.content: The following features will roll out over a four-day period starting March 17, 2026.

summary.title: New features rolling out starting March 17
summary.summary: New features will be available starting March 17, 2026. The rollout will take place over a four-day period.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where some drill links could fail to work when cookieless embed was enabled. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Drill links fixed with cookieless embed
summary.summary: An issue where drill links failed with cookieless embed enabled has been resolved. The feature now functions correctly.

2026-02-09.content: When you are [delivering content to an SFTP server](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/delivering-looks-explores#delivery_options_for_sftp_servers), additional key exchange algorithms and host key algorithms are now supported.

summary.title: SFTP delivery enhancements
summary.summary: SFTP delivery now supports additional key exchange and host key algorithms. This expands the options available when sending data to SFTP servers, improving compatibility and flexibility.

2026-02-09.content: When you use [Elite System Activity](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/elite-system-activity), the `merge_query` table now refreshes every 10 minutes.

summary.title: Elite System Activity Refresh Rate Increased
summary.summary: The `merge_query` table used with Elite System Activity now refreshes every 10 minutes, providing you with more up-to-date information. This change enhances the timeliness of your system activity data.

2026-02-09.content:
An issue has been fixed where the pagination option was not being displayed in the LookML Dashboards folder for some users.
This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Pagination displayed in LookML Dashboards folder
summary.summary: A bug that prevented the pagination option from displaying in the LookML Dashboards folder has been resolved. This feature now functions correctly for all users.

2026-02-09.content: The user attribute pairing user interface for [SAML](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-authentication-saml), [LDAP](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-authentication-ldap), and [OpenID Connect](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-authentication-openid-connect) authentication has been updated. A new "Manage Pairings" side panel provides a robust interface for adding, removing, and viewing attribute pairings. This new interface also includes filtering and pagination and allows for a single claim to be associated with multiple Looker user attributes.

summary.title: Updated User Attribute Pairing Interface
summary.summary: The user attribute pairing interface for SAML, LDAP, and OpenID Connect authentication has been updated with a new 'Manage Pairings' side panel. This provides a more robust way to manage attribute pairings, including filtering, pagination, and associating a single claim with multiple user attributes.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where custom dimensions that were based on numeric fields could be converted to strings, which caused incorrect sorting. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Numeric dimension sorting fixed
summary.summary: We've resolved an issue where custom dimensions based on numeric fields were incorrectly converted to strings, leading to sorting problems. The feature now functions as expected, ensuring accurate sorting of numeric data.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where choosing a non-default color collection and then choosing a custom color in a conditional formatting rule could cause the rule to revert to the default color collection. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting color issue resolved
summary.summary: An issue where conditional formatting rules incorrectly reverted to default colors after selecting custom options has been fixed. The feature now functions as expected, allowing users to apply custom color collections without unexpected changes.

2026-02-09.content: The [Customer Engineer Advanced Editor](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-users-roles#customer_engineer_advanced_editor) default role now includes the `gemini_in_looker`, `chat_with_agent`, `chat_with_explore`, and `save_agents` permissions, which grant access to Gemini features and Conversational Analytics functionality.

summary.title: Gemini Features Now Available
summary.summary: The Customer Engineer Advanced Editor role now includes permissions for Gemini features and Conversational Analytics. This update grants access to Gemini in Looker, agent chat, explore chat, and the ability to save agents.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where a modification to the color palette for the "Along a scale..." conditional formatting feature could fail to be saved. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting color palette fix
summary.summary: The issue where modifications to the color palette for the "Along a scale..." conditional formatting feature were not saved has been resolved. This feature now saves and functions correctly.

2026-02-09.content:
An issue has been fixed where non-admin users could not favorite
LookML dashboards. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Fixed LookML dashboard favoriting for non-admins
summary.summary: An issue preventing non-admin users from favoriting LookML dashboards has been resolved. This functionality now works correctly, allowing all users to save their preferred dashboards.

2026-02-09.content: Looker admins can now [grant essential Google Cloud services](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/admin-panel-server-ip-allowlist), such as [Conversational Analytics](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/conversational-analytics-overview), access to a Looker instance, even when an IP allowlist is active.

summary.title: Allow essential services through IP allowlists
summary.summary: Looker admins can now grant essential Google Cloud services, like Conversational Analytics, access to a Looker instance even when an IP allowlist is active. This ensures critical features remain accessible without compromising security.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where Looker could return a 500 error if a user with one or more single quotes in their name attempted to commit LookML. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: LookML commit error resolved
summary.summary: An issue causing Looker to return a 500 error when users with single quotes in their names committed LookML has been fixed. The feature now functions correctly.

2026-02-09.content: Now available in preview, Looker has full support for connections with [AlloyDB for PostgreSQL](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/db-config-alloydb). When you [create a connection](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/connecting-to-your-db) in Looker, you can now select "Google Cloud AlloyDB for PostgreSQL" from the Dialect drop-down menu. This update does not affect existing AlloyDB connections that were created using the PostgreSQL 9.5+ option in the Dialect menu.

summary.title: Looker Now Supports AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
summary.summary: Looker now offers full support for connections with AlloyDB for PostgreSQL in preview. Users can select 'Google Cloud AlloyDB for PostgreSQL' from the Dialect drop-down menu when creating a new connection in Looker. This enhancement simplifies integration for users of both services.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where switching a visualization type from Table to Single Value could carry over unwanted conditional formatting. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting issue resolved
summary.summary: An issue where conditional formatting persisted between visualization types has been fixed. The feature now functions correctly, ensuring a seamless user experience when switching between Table and Single Value visualizations.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where conditional formatting no longer appeared on certain dashboard tiles. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting now visible on dashboard tiles
summary.summary: An issue that prevented conditional formatting from appearing on certain dashboard tiles has been resolved. The feature now functions correctly, ensuring that conditional formatting is displayed as expected.

2026-02-09.content: The Looker Action Hub has been updated to support newer API versions for Google Ads (from v19 to v22) and Facebook Custom Audiences (from v22 to v24).

summary.title: Looker Action Hub API Updates
summary.summary: The Looker Action Hub now supports newer API versions for Google Ads and Facebook Custom Audiences. This update ensures compatibility and access to the latest features for these integrations.

2026-02-09.content:
The **Increase SQL Runner Schema and Table Limit** Labs feature is now available in private preview.

**Note:** This item was added on February 20, 2026 and updated on March 2, 2026.

summary.title: SQL Runner Schema and Table Limit Increased
summary.summary: The Labs feature to increase the SQL Runner schema and table limit is now available in private preview. This enhancement allows for greater flexibility when querying larger datasets.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where changing the collection in a conditional formatting rule could reset styles for the rule. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting styles preserved
summary.summary: An issue where changing the collection in a conditional formatting rule could reset styles has been fixed. The feature now performs as expected, ensuring your styles are maintained.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where the filter bar would automatically expand on dashboard load when the filter location was set to "Right". This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Filter bar behavior corrected
summary.summary: An issue has been fixed where the filter bar would automatically expand on dashboard load when the filter location was set to "Right". This feature now performs as expected.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where switching filter types from `Matches (Advanced)` to another filter type could populate that filter with an incorrect filter configuration. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Filter configuration issue resolved
summary.summary: An issue where switching filter types could lead to incorrect filter configurations has been fixed. The feature now operates as expected, ensuring accurate filter settings.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where adding a new field to a conditionally formatted result set could fail to apply the conditional formatting to the new field. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Conditional formatting now applies to new fields
summary.summary: An issue where new fields in conditionally formatted result sets failed to apply formatting has been resolved. This feature now functions correctly, ensuring all new fields are properly formatted.

2026-02-09.content: An issue has been fixed where dashboard tiles with titles that included the % symbol could not be downloaded in the Safari 26.0 browser. This feature now performs as expected.

summary.title: Dashboard tile download issue resolved
summary.summary: An issue preventing dashboard tiles with titles containing the '%' symbol from being downloaded in Safari 26.0 has been fixed. This functionality now works correctly.

2026-02-09.content: When you use [Elite System Activity](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/elite-system-activity), all System Activity fields of the `longtext` type that have a size greater than 2 MB will be truncated. If a table has only one column with a `longtext` type, that column will be set to a maximum of 1.9 MB in size. If a table has multiple columns of the `longtext` type, the total maximum size across all such columns is 2 MB, and this limit is distributed uniformly among those columns.
